name: From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga
name_original: De Estarreja à Ponte do poço de Santiago — percurso circular passando na Ecopista do Vouga
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Beduído traverse a landscape characterized by coastal plains, riverine paths, and areas of dune forest. The region features a mix of flat sections, such as those found along the Ecopista do Vouga, and more challenging climbs indicated by significant elevation gains on some routes. This varied terrain provides diverse road cycling experiences, from long-distance endurance rides to routes exploring local cultural landmarks. Best road cycling routes around Beduído The most popular road cycling route is From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga, a 41.9 miles (67.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route includes sections along the Ecopista do Vouga. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Margres Aveiro Spring Classic – Carcavelos Bridge loop from Estarreja, a difficult 94.7 miles (152.4 km) path. This extensive loop offers a long-distance challenge across varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Dune Forest of Ovar – Ponte da Varela loop from Estarreja, a 63.9 miles (102.9 km) trail leading through dune forest areas, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Beduído is defined by coastal plains, river paths, and dune forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Beduído are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Beduído's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Beduído?
A: Beduído offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 100 tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Beduído?
A: Yes, Beduído has routes suitable for beginners. There are 11 easy routes available, offering a gentle introduction to road cycling in the region. These typically feature flatter sections, such as those found along the Ecopista do Vouga.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of road cycling routes in Beduído?
A: Route lengths and durations vary significantly. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384002830/from-estarreja-to-ponte-do-poco-de-santiago-circular-route-passing-on-the-ecopista-do-vouga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga&lt;/a&gt; is about 41.9 miles (67.4 km) and takes around 3 hours 32 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Margres Aveiro Spring Classic – Carcavelos Bridge loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; extend to 94.7 miles (152.4 km).

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Beduído?
A: Yes, Beduído's varied terrain includes challenging climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24543836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban Art Murals of Estarreja – Marnel Medieval Bridge loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial elevation gains, providing a good test for advanced cyclists.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect along the road cycling routes in Beduído?
A: Road cycling routes in Beduído offer diverse scenery, including coastal plains, riverine paths, and areas of dune forest. You can also pass by attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Canal 🌊 Aveiro Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EV1 on the Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Beduído?
A: Yes, many routes in Beduído are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384002830/from-estarreja-to-ponte-do-poco-de-santiago-circular-route-passing-on-the-ecopista-do-vouga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Estarreja to Ponte do poço de Santiago — circular route passing on the Ecopista do Vouga&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular option.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can discover landmarks such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4719493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcavelos Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2924364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6311238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igreja Matriz de Oliveira de Azeméis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Beduído?
A: The road cycling routes in Beduído are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes coastal plains, river paths, and dune forests, offering diverse experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Beduído?
A: While specific data isn't provided, coastal regions like Beduído generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring more rain, but cycling is possible year-round.

Q: Are there routes that pass through dune forests?
A: Yes, Beduído's landscape includes dune forest areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32192229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dune Forest of Ovar – Ponte da Varela loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that takes you through these unique natural environments.

Q: Can I find routes that take me along the coast or near beaches?
A: Yes, the region's coastal plains mean many routes offer views or access to coastal areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32298378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Varela – Praia do Furadouro loop from Murtosa&lt;/a&gt; will bring you close to the beach, allowing you to experience the coastal scenery.
tips:
Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau

Pinheiro da Bemposta, is located between the Antuã and Caima rivers. Its history goes back to D. Teresa, mother of D. Afonso Henriques. The Cruzeiro do Pinheiro da Bemposta, right in the center of the village, and the Chapel of Nossa Senhora da Ribeira stand out. Pinheiro da Bemposta is on the Portuguese route to Santiago.

The Paradela Railway Station served the towns of Sever do Vouga and Paradela. Opened in 1913, the station closed in 1990 and subsequently fell into ruin. In 2011, Sociedade Polis Litoral Ria de Aveiro began its recovery, transforming it into a support hub for the Linha do Vouga eco-trail. Today it offers changing rooms, bicycle rental and repair, an environmental interpretation center, restaurant and terrace, making it a strategic stopping point for cyclists traveling through the region.

The Poço de S. Tiago Bridge, built between 1912 and 1913, is a remarkable 165-meter engineering work along the Vouga River. Integrated into the Vale do Vouga Line, closed in 1990, it was converted into an eco-track, revitalizing the old Paradela station as a tourist attraction.

For cyclists on road bikes: be careful, the bridge is passable, but the surface is gravel. It does not present significant technical difficulties, but it is important to take this into account.

Albergaria-a-Velha has a long tradition associated with pilgrimage (hence the name) and is part of the current Portuguese route to Santiago de Compostela. The central square is charming and an ideal stopping point on a bike ride.

waypoints.name: Statue of Queen D. Teresa in Albergaria-a-Velha
